entries.lesson: When a thematic inflection (e.g., generative-AI post-ChatGPT) is actively re-rating the semiconductor cohort, companies whose management quantify specific dollar exposure to the theme (e.g., AVGO: AI-Ethernet $200M→$800M+, compute-offload $2B→$3B+) receive outsized positive reactions even on modest headline beats — route predictions in Technology to weight theme-$ quantification above traditional EPS-beat magnitude whenever a sector benchmark name has just printed a related guide-up.

For the 2023 AI-accelerator/custom-silicon cohort, AVGO and MRVL fates diverge on the AI-exposure vs inventory-correction axis: AVGO's +5.54% and MRVL's -4.74% on the same day (2023-03-03) under a supportive semi tape (SOXX +1.54%) show that peer prints are evaluated on thematic positioning, not on whether they 'beat'. When two of these three co-report, use the other's qualitative AI commentary (not just beat/miss) as a differentiator.

In thematic-momentum regimes where one benchmark name's earnings inflects investor attention (here NVDA 2023-02-22), subsequent same-theme earnings events deliver asymmetric returns: guide-above-consensus PLUS theme-quantification → outsized positive; guide-below or theme-silent → outsized negative. The asymmetry is visible in the 2-3 weeks after the benchmark print and should adjust the predictor's prior on magnitude (widen expected range, favor directional calls).

In an active AI-rerating regime, the decisive forward-narrative dollar quantification by hyperscaler-exposed semis (AI revenue $/quarter, mix-progression trajectory, networking-vs-ASIC composition) is delivered on the live earnings call rather than in the 8-K press release; sector-wide, weight the call commentary far above the press-release headline language for any semi/networking name with hyperscaler exposure.

AVGO and MRVL share an AI-networking merchant-silicon thesis (Tomahawk/Jericho vs Marvell's Inphi/Innovium DSP/PAM4 portfolio). When AVGO confirms hyperscaler AI-Ethernet ramp on its call, treat it as a positive read-through to MRVL's next print specifically (not a generalized semi tape signal — e.g., NVDA, AMD, SMCI all traded RED on 6/2 while AVGO ripped, proving the read-through is networking-specific, not AI-broad).

When stocks rally hard into a thematic-regime print (here +32% in 3 months, capped by a flurry of late-stage PT hikes), the ACTUAL print-day adjusted move (-3.18% on 6/1) is positioning de-risk, not the market verdict — the verdict is the analyst PT wave in the next morning's pre-open. Predictors using day-of-release intra-day weakness as a confirmation signal of the bear thesis will systematically miscall this regime.

For high-multiple semiconductor names that have been re-rated on a thematic growth narrative (AI in 2023, but the mechanism generalizes), an earnings event where management affirms the theme AND admits every non-theme segment is declining YoY tends to derate the stock even on a headline beat — because the market reprices the durability of the growth stack. Segment-concentration disclosure is a compression catalyst that is orthogonal to EPS or revenue surprise.

When sell-side analysts raise price targets aggressively INTO a high-multiple semi print (three $900-$1010 PT revisions in the week before AVGO), the cushion between reported outcome and priced outcome collapses — any call-level incremental negative then amplifies into outsized next-session drawdowns despite post-print PT raises. PT-hike velocity in the final week pre-print is a useful tail-risk indicator for magnitude, not just direction.

In an active thematic re-rating regime, management's CALL-LEVEL narrative-perimeter statements — statements that EXPAND the theme's TAM (Q1/Q2 FY23 AI $-quantifications) versus statements that CAP it ('I have one hyperscaler, leave it at that') — are the dominant driver of post-print reaction, frequently overwhelming the sign and magnitude of the reported EPS/revenue surprise. Post-print attribution should explicitly identify whether the call expanded or contracted the theme's narrative boundary.
